title: ByteWolf Cognitive Runtime Tool Trace Entry v0.1
description: One record of a tool (plugin capability) call made during a turn. It carries a REFERENCE to the arguments, never the raw arguments, so the trace stays auditable without leaking prompt content or sensitive input. A tool_trace entry is a component of a response envelope, not a standalone document, so it does not repeat the envelope's contract_version. See docs/workstreams/cognitive-runtime.md.

call_id.description: Unique id for this call within the turn, so a trace entry can be correlated with logs without exposing its payload.

capability_id.description: The plugin capability that was invoked, e.g. 'telemetry.read'. Matches the Plugin SDK capability namespace.

status.description: Outcome of the call. 'ok': returned normally. 'denied': the ToolPolicy did not grant it. 'error': the capability raised. 'timeout': the call exceeded its ToolPolicy limit and was cancelled.

latency_ms.description: Wall-clock milliseconds the call took, from dispatch to outcome.

args_ref.description: An opaque reference to the arguments (e.g. a content hash), NEVER the raw arguments. The trace records that a call happened and with which argument shape, not what the argument values were.

detail.description: Free text for audit on a non-ok status, e.g. a denial reason or an error class. Never parsed for dispatch, and never the raw tool output.

then.description: detail explains a non-ok outcome; an ok call carries none, so the trace cannot smuggle raw output through it.
